stages.title: Intake
stages.sub: Client sends a DDQ; the platform parses it into questions

user.value: Client RM at a pension fund
emails a 180-question AFME custodian DDQ as an Excel attachment to
, copying the BNY relationship manager. They expect a response in 3 weeks.

system.value: Intake worker
receives the message via Microsoft Graph subscription, downloads the attachment to S3, runs Unstructured.io to extract questions, and creates a
Run
with a parsed question list. The RM gets a confirmation in their inbox within 60 seconds.

stages.title: Classify
stages.sub: Each incoming question gets mapped to a canonical ID

user.value: The RM and the client are waiting. Internally, an SME may later see flagged unmapped questions in their queue, but most questions classify automatically.

system.value: QuestionMapper agent

(Haiku) embeds each question, retrieves top-k candidate canonical IDs from Qdrant, reranks, and assigns. High confidence mappings auto-confirm; low confidence routes to an SME for one-click confirmation. AFME-IS-3.4 →
canon.is.access_control.privileged_access_review

body.value: row written if new; existing mappings reused
event with confidence score per question

stages.title: Retrieve
stages.sub: Find the right answer — from the library if one exists, or from evidence

user.value: SMEs may see a small queue of questions where retrieval surfaced low-confidence matches needing human review.

system.value: For each canonical_id, library lookup runs first.
Cache hit → reuse the approved answer (target: 92% of questions). Miss →
EvidenceSourcer agent
hits the hybrid retrieval pipeline: BM25 + dense + Cohere rerank against the corpus, filtered to the right entity and within freshness windows.

body.value: searched on miss; entity-scoped at the database layer
with full ranked list, not just what was used

stages.title: Draft
stages.sub: Compose a response, anchored to evidence

user.value: By now the orchestrator is fanning out across all 180 questions in parallel batches.

system.value: DraftComposer agent
(Sonnet for tier-2/3, Opus for tier-1) writes prose constrained by the evidence bundle. Every factual claim must cite a
(doc_hash, span_hash)
tuple. Library hits skip drafting entirely — the approved text is reused verbatim.

body.value: from the live entry is used unmodified
Cited spans tracked by hash, not span_id (stable across reparses)
events with prompt_hash, response_hash, model, params

stages.title: Validate
stages.sub: Run all four guardrails before anything ships

user.value: Validation halts surface in SME queues
when a guardrail fails: "3 questions blocked — citation missing", "5 questions stale — SOC report expired". The SME sees the draft, the evidence, and the failed check side-by-side.

system.value: Validator service

evaluates four OPA policies in parallel: citation resolution, evidence freshness, cross-DDQ consistency, confidentiality scrub. Single fail → halt or escalate. Cross-DDQ check queries DuckDB over the response register for the same canonical_id shipped in the last 90 days.

body.value: scanned for divergent answers to same
consulted; SOC < 12 months, ISO within validity
events; failures sunk to SIEM within 30s

stages.title: Approve
stages.sub: SME and legal sign off where needed; tier-2/3 auto-pass

user.value: by domain (infosec, ops, regulatory, ESG, cyber, legal). Each item: question, draft, evidence, validation report. They click approve, edit, reject, or escalate. Tier-1 questions also flow to legal review. Auto-pass target: ~70% for tier-2/3.

system.value: ApprovalRouter

uses OPA policy to determine the required approver set per tier. Decisions flow back into the orchestrator graph. Edits loop back to validation. Approved entries can be promoted into the library so the next DDQ benefits.

body.value: event with named user, role, timestamp, comment

user.value: Client RM receives the completed DDQ

as a downloadable package — the original questionnaire format with answers filled in, plus citations to BNY public material where required. Optionally signed via DocuSign for formal sign-off. The client portal shows a full audit trail on request.

system.value: Orchestrator seals the run.

Every event, prompt, retrieval, decision, and artifact bundled. Merkle root computed. Bundle written to S3 with Object Lock. Response register populated for future consistency checks. Sealed run is bit-exact reproducible from the journal alone.

body.value: recorded so historical replay finds the exact version cited
